title: The Cube Chronicles
author: Jeremy Tinder
shortDescription: The Cube Chronicles is a fifteen-book middle-grade series in which three siblings from a close-knit, faith-filled family discover a mysterious nine-sided cube beneath their backyard shed — a cube that carries them across the ancient world, from the building of the Great Pyramid to the crossroads of the Silk Road, one civilization and one hard-won lesson at a time.

family.name: The Carver family
family.home: Oakdale, the rural Carolinas (contemporary frame story beginning in 1986)

members.name: Daniel Carver
members.role: Father; a builder and carpenter whose craft and yearly "wisdoms" frame each journey

members.name: Elizabeth Carver
members.role: Mother; a Filipina-American homemaker (from Pampanga) who keeps the family's traditions of faith and hospitality

members.name: Simeon Carver
members.role: Eldest child; a builder like his father who apprentices in a craft on every crossing

members.name: Beckah Carver
members.role: Middle child; the family's record-keeper, reader, and decoder of the cube's signs

members.name: Ellie Carver
members.role: Youngest child; the family's singer and listener, carrier of the songs gathered along the way

premise: A nine-sided cube, discovered beneath the floorboards of an old shed, warms and glows a new configuration roughly once a year. When it activates, it carries the Carver children into a real ancient civilization, where they live, learn a craft, make friends, and come home changed. The series follows the children and their parents across fifteen years and fifteen destinations toward the still-unbuilt center of a spiral the cube has been drawing all along.

settingsInOrder.place: Ancient Egypt (the Nile Valley, the Great Pyramid of Giza)
Classical Athens (the Parthenon and the Acropolis)
settingsInOrder.era: Golden Age of Athens, 5th century BCE

settingsInOrder.place: A Yellow River highland village (early Bronze Age China)
settingsInOrder.era: Early Bronze Age China, c. 1900 BCE

settingsInOrder.place: A Jōmon fishing and pottery village (ancient Japan)
The city of Ur (ancient Sumer, Mesopotamia)
A high terraced village in the Andes
The songline country of Aboriginal Australia
The center of the journey — the road home

makerNote: A guiding, unseen presence the family calls "the Maker" is spoken of with reverence across the series but is never depicted or shown as a visible character. The website preserves that restraint.
